sections.heading: Required Access
sections.body:
To view cloud backup restore jobs, you must have [Project Backup Recovery Operator](<Project Backup Recovery Operator>), [Project Backup Manager](<Project Backup Manager>), or
[Project Owner](<Project Owner>) access to the project.

sections.heading: Limitations
sections.body:
You can't restore a snapshot created
from a MongoDB 7.0 sharded cluster with 3 or fewer shards
to a MongoDB 8.0 sharded cluster with 3 or fewer shards
with the default [dedicated config server](config-server-types) configuration on MongoDB 8.0.
You must first disable the [Atlas-Managed Config Servers for Sharded Clusters](config-server-management)
to create a sharded cluster with dedicated config server before restoring the snapshot.

sections.heading: Stop Client Operations during Restoration
sections.body:
You must ensure that the target Atlas cluster
doesn't receive client requests during restoration. You can restore to
a new cluster and reconfigure your application to use
that new cluster once it is running for maximum uptime.

sections.heading: View Cluster Restore Jobs
sections.body:
To list all cloud backup restore jobs for your cluster using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas backups restores describe <restoreJobId> [options]

```

To return the details for the cloud backup restore job you specify using the Atlas CLI, run the following command:

```sh

atlas accessLists create [entry] [options]

```

To learn more about the syntax and parameters for the previous commands, see the Atlas CLI documentation for
[atlas backups restores describe](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-backups-restores-describe) and
[atlas accessLists create](atlas/cli/current/command/atlas-accessLists-create).

Related Links

- [Install the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/install-atlas-cli)

- [Connect to the Atlas CLI](atlas/cli/current/connect-atlas-cli)

Atlas UI

Atlas provides a detailed list of completed and in-progress
snapshot restorations, including when Atlas took the snapshot and
the snapshot's delivery type. To view this list:

The Status column of the table displays the results of
completed snapshots, and the progress of snapshots currently
being restored.

- For manually downloaded snapshots, the Status column
  displays progress while Atlas prepares the download link. Once
  the download is ready, the column displays the link to download the
  snapshot.

- For automated restores and Continuous Cloud Backup restores, the
  Status column updates as the restoration progresses on
  each node in the cluster. When the restoration completes, the column
  displays the time of completion and the cluster to which Atlas
  restored the snapshot.

See also [Troubleshoot Encryption at Rest](troubleshoot-snapshot-encryption).
